   323→// runEnableInteractive runs the interactive enable flow.
   324→func runEnableInteractive(w io.Writer, localDev, _, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   325→	// Check if already fully enabled — show summary and return early.
   326→	// Skip early return if any configuration flags are set (user wants to reconfigure).
   327→	hasConfigFlags := forceHooks || skipPushSessions || !telemetry || useLocalSettings || useProjectSettings || localDev
   328→	if !hasConfigFlags {
   329→		if fullyEnabled, agentDesc, configPath := isFullyEnabled(); fullyEnabled {
   330→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "Already enabled. Everything looks good.")
   331→			fmt.Fprintln(w)
   332→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Agent: %s\n", agentDesc)
   333→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Config: %s\n", configPath)
   334→			return nil
   335→		}
   336→	}
   337→
   338→	// Detect default agent
   339→	ag := agent.Default()
   340→	agentType := string(agent.AgentTypeClaudeCode)
   341→	if ag != nil {
   342→		agentType = string(ag.Type())
   343→	}
   344→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s (use --agent to change)\n\n", agentType)
   345→
   346→	// Setup Claude Code hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   347→	if _, err := setupClaudeCodeHook(localDev, forceHooks); err != nil {
   348→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup Claude Code hooks: %w", err)
   349→	}
   350→
   351→	// Setup .entire directory
   352→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   353→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   354→	}
   355→
   356→	// Use the default strategy (manual-commit)
   357→	internalStrategy := strategy.DefaultStrategyName
   358→
   359→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   360→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   361→	if err != nil {
   362→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   363→		settings = &EntireSettings{}
   364→	}
   365→	// Update the specific fields
   366→	settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   367→	settings.LocalDev = localDev
   368→	settings.Enabled = true
   369→
   370→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   371→	if skipPushSessions {
   372→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   373→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   374→		}
   375→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   376→	}
   377→
   378→	// Determine which settings file to write to
   379→	// First run always creates settings.json (no prompt)
   380→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   381→	if err != nil {
   382→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   383→	}
   384→	shouldUseLocal, showNotification := determineSettingsTarget(entireDirAbs, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings)
   385→
   386→	if showNotification {
   387→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Info: Project settings exist. Saving to settings.local.json instead.")
   388→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Use --project to update the project settings file.")
   389→	}
   390→
   391→	// Helper to save settings to the appropriate file
   392→	saveSettings := func() error {
   393→		if shouldUseLocal {
   394→			return SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings)
   395→		}
   396→		return SaveEntireSettings(settings)
   397→	}
   398→
   399→	// Save settings before telemetry prompt so config is persisted even if the user cancels
   400→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   401→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   402→	}
   403→
   404→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   405→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   406→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   407→	}
   408→	strategy.CheckAndWarnHookManagers(w)
   409→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "✓ Hooks installed")
   410→
   411→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   412→	if shouldUseLocal {
   413→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   414→	}
   415→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplay)
   416→
   417→	// Ask about telemetry consent (only if not already asked)
   418→	fmt.Fprintln(w)
   419→	if err := promptTelemetryConsent(settings, telemetry); err != nil {
   420→		return fmt.Errorf("telemetry consent: %w", err)
   421→	}
   422→	// Save again to persist telemetry choice
   423→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   424→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   425→	}
   426→
   427→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements
   428→	strat, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy)
   429→	if err != nil {
   430→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   431→	}
   432→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   433→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   434→	}
   435→
   436→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   437→
   438→	return nil
   439→}

   441→// runEnable is a simple enable that just sets the enabled flag (for programmatic use).
   442→func runEnable(w io.Writer) error {
   443→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   444→	if err != nil {
   445→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load settings: %w", err)
   446→	}
   447→
   448→	settings.Enabled = true
   449→	if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   450→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "Entire is now enabled.")
   454→	return nil
   455→}

   457→func runDisable(w io.Writer, useProjectSettings bool) error {
   458→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   459→	if err != nil {
   460→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load settings: %w", err)
   461→	}
   462→
   463→	settings.Enabled = false
   464→
   465→	// If --project flag is specified, always write to project settings
   466→	if useProjectSettings {
   467→		if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   468→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   469→		}
   470→	} else {
   471→		// Always write to local settings file (create if doesn't exist)
   472→		if err := SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings); err != nil {
   473→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save local settings: %w", err)
   474→		}
   475→	}
   476→
   477→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "Entire is now disabled.")
   478→	return nil
   479→}

   481→// DisabledMessage is the message shown when Entire is disabled
   482→const DisabledMessage = "Entire is disabled. Run `entire enable` to re-enable."
   483→
   484→// checkDisabledGuard checks if Entire is disabled and prints a message if so.
   485→// Returns true if the caller should exit (i.e., Entire is disabled).
   486→// On error reading settings, defaults to enabled (returns false).
   487→func checkDisabledGuard(w io.Writer) bool {
   488→	enabled, err := IsEnabled()
   489→	if err != nil {
   490→		// Default to enabled on error
   491→		return false
   492→	}
   493→	if !enabled {
   494→		fmt.Fprintln(w, DisabledMessage)
   495→		return true
   496→	}
   497→	return false
   498→}

   500→// setupClaudeCodeHook sets up Claude Code hooks.
   501→// This is a convenience wrapper that uses the agent package.
   502→// Returns the number of hooks installed (0 if already installed).
   503→func setupClaudeCodeHook(localDev, forceHooks bool) (int, error) { //nolint:unparam // already present in codebase
   504→	ag, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameClaudeCode)
   505→	if err != nil {
   506→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to get claude-code agent: %w", err)
   507→	}
   508→
   509→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
   510→	if !ok {
   511→		return 0, errors.New("claude-code agent does not support hooks")
   512→	}
   513→
   514→	count, err := hookAgent.InstallHooks(localDev, forceHooks)
   515→	if err != nil {
   516→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to install claude-code hooks: %w", err)
   517→	}
   518→
   519→	return count, nil
   520→}

   522→// printAgentError writes an error message followed by available agents and usage.
   523→func printAgentError(w io.Writer, message string) {
   524→	agents := agent.List()
   525→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s Available agents:\n", message)
   526→	fmt.Fprintln(w)
   527→	for _, a := range agents {
   528→		suffix := ""
   529→		if a == agent.DefaultAgentName {
   530→			suffix = "    (default)"
   531→		}
   532→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %s%s\n", a, suffix)
   533→	}
   534→	fmt.Fprintln(w)
   535→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "Usage: entire enable --agent <agent-name>")
   536→}

   539→func printMissingAgentError(w io.Writer) {

   548→// setupAgentHooksNonInteractive sets up hooks for a specific agent non-interactively.
   549→// If strategyName is provided, it sets the strategy; otherwise uses default.
   550→func setupAgentHooksNonInteractive(w io.Writer, ag agent.Agent, strategyName string, localDev, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   551→	agentName := ag.Name()
   552→	// Check if agent supports hooks
   553→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
   554→	if !ok {
   555→		return fmt.Errorf("agent %s does not support hooks", agentName)
   556→	}
   557→
   558→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s\n\n", ag.Type())
   559→
   560→	// Install agent hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   561→	installedHooks, err := hookAgent.InstallHooks(localDev, forceHooks)
   562→	if err != nil {
   563→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install hooks for %s: %w", agentName, err)
   564→	}
   565→
   566→	// Setup .entire directory
   567→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   568→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   569→	}
   570→
   571→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   572→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   573→	if err != nil {
   574→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   575→		settings = &EntireSettings{Strategy: strategy.DefaultStrategyName}
   576→	}
   577→	settings.Enabled = true
   578→	if localDev {
   579→		settings.LocalDev = localDev
   580→	}
   581→
   582→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   583→	if skipPushSessions {
   584→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   585→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   586→		}
   587→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   588→	}
   589→
   590→	// Set strategy if provided
   591→	if strategyName != "" {
   592→		// Map display name to internal name if needed
   593→		internalStrategy := strategyName
   594→		if mapped, ok := strategyDisplayToInternal[strategyName]; ok {
   595→			internalStrategy = mapped
   596→		}
   597→		// Validate the strategy exists
   598→		if _, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy); err != nil {
   599→			return fmt.Errorf("unknown strategy: %s (use manual-commit or auto-commit)", strategyName)
   600→		}
   601→		settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   602→	}
   603→
   604→	// Handle telemetry for non-interactive mode
   605→	// Note: if telemetry is nil (not configured), it defaults to disabled
   606→	if !telemetry || os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   607→		f := false
   608→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   609→	}
   610→
   611→	if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   612→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   613→	}
   614→
   615→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   616→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   617→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   618→	}
   619→	strategy.CheckAndWarnHookManagers(w)
   620→
   621→	if installedHooks == 0 {
   622→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Hooks for %s already installed", ag.Description())
   623→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   624→			msg += " (Preview)"
   625→		}
   626→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   627→	} else {
   628→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Installed %d hooks for %s", installedHooks, ag.Description())
   629→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   630→			msg += " (Preview)"
   631→		}
   632→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   633→	}
   634→
   635→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplayProject)
   636→
   637→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements (creates entire/checkpoints/v1 branch, etc.)
   638→	strat, err := strategy.Get(settings.Strategy)
   639→	if err != nil {
   640→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   641→	}
   642→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   643→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   644→	}
   645→
   646→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   647→
   648→	return nil
   649→}
   650→
   651→// validateSetupFlags checks that --local and --project flags are not both specified.
   652→func validateSetupFlags(useLocal, useProject bool) error {
   653→	if useLocal && useProject {
   654→		return errors.New("cannot specify both --project and --local")
   655→	}
   656→	return nil
   657→}
   658→
   659→// determineSettingsTarget decides whether to write to settings.local.json based on:
   660→// - Whether settings.json already exists
   661→// - The --local and --project flags
   662→// Returns (useLocal, showNotification).
   663→func determineSettingsTarget(entireDir string, useLocal, useProject bool) (bool, bool) {
   664→	// Explicit --local flag always uses local settings
   665→	if useLocal {
   666→		return true, false
   667→	}
   668→
   669→	// Explicit --project flag always uses project settings
   670→	if useProject {
   671→		return false, false
   672→	}
   673→
   674→	// No flags specified - check if settings file exists
   675→	settingsPath := filepath.Join(entireDir, paths.SettingsFileName)
   676→	if _, err := os.Stat(settingsPath); err == nil {
   677→		// Settings file exists - auto-redirect to local with notification
   678→		return true, true
   679→	}
   680→
   681→	// Settings file doesn't exist - create it
   682→	return false, false
   683→}
   684→
   685→// setupEntireDirectory creates the .entire directory and gitignore.
   686→// Returns true if the directory was created, false if it already existed.
   687→func setupEntireDirectory() (bool, error) { //nolint:unparam // already present in codebase
   688→	// Get absolute path for the .entire directory
   689→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   690→	if err != nil {
   691→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   692→	}
   693→
   694→	// Check if directory already exists
   695→	created := false
   696→	if _, err := os.Stat(entireDirAbs); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   697→		created = true
   698→	}
   699→
   700→	// Create .entire directory
   701→	//nolint:gosec // G301: Project directory needs standard permissions for git
   702→	if err := os.MkdirAll(entireDirAbs, 0o755); err != nil {
   703→		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to create .entire directory: %w", err)
   704→	}
   705→
   706→	// Create/update .gitignore with all required entries
   707→	if err := strategy.EnsureEntireGitignore(); err != nil {
   708→		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .gitignore: %w", err)
   709→	}
   710→
   711→	return created, nil
   712→}
   713→
   714→// setupGitHook installs the prepare-commit-msg hook for context trailers.
   715→func setupGitHook() error {
   716→	// Use shared implementation from strategy package
   717→	// The localDev setting is read from settings.json
   718→	_, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(false) // not silent - show output during setup
   719→	if err != nil {
   720→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hook: %w", err)
   721→	}
   722→	strategy.CheckAndWarnHookManagers(os.Stderr)
   723→	return nil
   724→}
   725→
   726→// newSetupGitHookCmd creates the standalone git-hook setup command
   727→func newSetupGitHookCmd() *cobra.Command {
   728→	cmd := &cobra.Command{
   729→		Use:    "git-hook",
   730→		Short:  "Install git hook for session context trailers",
   731→		Hidden: true, // Hidden as it's mainly for testing
   732→		RunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
   733→			return setupGitHook()
   734→		},
   735→	}
   736→
   737→	return cmd
   738→}
   739→
   740→func newCurlBashPostInstallCmd() *cobra.Command {
   741→	return &cobra.Command{
   742→		Use:    "curl-bash-post-install",
   743→		Short:  "Post-install tasks for curl|bash installer",
   744→		Hidden: true,
   745→		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
   746→			w := cmd.OutOrStdout()
   747→			if err := promptShellCompletion(w); err != nil {
   748→				fmt.Fprintf(w, "Note: Shell completion setup skipped: %v\n", err)
   749→			}
   750→			return nil
   751→		},
   752→	}
   753→}
   754→
   755→// shellCompletionComment is the comment preceding the completion line
   756→const shellCompletionComment = "# Entire CLI shell completion"
   757→
   758→// errUnsupportedShell is returned when the user's shell is not supported for completion.
   759→var errUnsupportedShell = errors.New("unsupported shell")
   760→
   761→// shellCompletionTarget returns the rc file path and completion lines for the
   762→// user's current shell.
   763→func shellCompletionTarget() (shellName, rcFile, completionLine string, err error) {
   764→	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   765→	if err != nil {
   766→		return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot determine home directory: %w", err)
   767→	}
   768→
   769→	shell := os.Getenv("SHELL")
   770→	switch {
   771→	case strings.Contains(shell, "zsh"):
   772→		return "Zsh",
   773→			filepath.Join(home, ".zshrc"),
   774→			"autoload -Uz compinit && compinit && source <(entire completion zsh)",
   775→			nil
   776→	case strings.Contains(shell, "bash"):
   777→		bashRC := filepath.Join(home, ".bashrc")
   778→		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")); err == nil {
   779→			bashRC = filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")
   780→		}
   781→		return "Bash",
   782→			bashRC,
   783→			"source <(entire completion bash)",
   784→			nil
   785→	case strings.Contains(shell, "fish"):
   786→		return "Fish",
   787→			filepath.Join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish"),
   788→			"entire completion fish | source",
   789→			nil
   790→	default:
   791→		return "", "", "", errUnsupportedShell
   792→	}
   793→}
   794→
   795→// promptShellCompletion offers to add shell completion to the user's rc file.
   796→// Only prompts if completion is not already configured.
   797→func promptShellCompletion(w io.Writer) error {
   798→	shellName, rcFile, completionLine, err := shellCompletionTarget()
   799→	if err != nil {
   800→		if errors.Is(err, errUnsupportedShell) {
   801→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "Note: Shell completion not available for your shell. Supported: zsh, bash, fish.\n")
   802→			return nil
   803→		}
   804→		return fmt.Errorf("shell completion: %w", err)
   805→	}
   806→
   807→	if isCompletionConfigured(rcFile) {
   808→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Shell completion already configured in %s\n", rcFile)
   809→		return nil
   810→	}
   811→
   812→	var selected string
   813→	form := NewAccessibleForm(
   814→		huh.NewGroup(
   815→			huh.NewSelect[string]().
   816→				Title(fmt.Sprintf("Enable shell completion? (detected: %s)", shellName)).
   817→				Options(
   818→					huh.NewOption("Yes", "yes"),
   819→					huh.NewOption("No", "no"),
   820→				).
   821→				Value(&selected),
   822→		),
   823→	)
   824→
   825→	if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   826→		//nolint:nilerr // User cancelled - not a fatal error, just skip
   827→		return nil
   828→	}
   829→
   830→	if selected != "yes" {
   831→		return nil
   832→	}
   833→
   834→	if err := appendShellCompletion(rcFile, completionLine); err != nil {
   835→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update %s: %w", rcFile, err)
   836→	}
   837→
   838→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Shell completion added to %s\n", rcFile)
   839→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Restart your shell to activate")
   840→
   841→	return nil
   842→}
   843→
   844→// isCompletionConfigured checks if shell completion is already in the rc file.
   845→func isCompletionConfigured(rcFile string) bool {
   846→	//nolint:gosec // G304: rcFile is constructed from home dir + known filename, not user input
   847→	content, err := os.ReadFile(rcFile)
   848→	if err != nil {
   849→		return false // File doesn't exist or can't read, treat as not configured
   850→	}
   851→	return strings.Contains(string(content), "entire completion")
   852→}
   853→
   854→// appendShellCompletion adds the completion line to the rc file.
   855→func appendShellCompletion(rcFile, completionLine string) error {
   856→	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(rcFile), 0o700); err != nil {
   857→		return fmt.Errorf("creating directory: %w", err)
   858→	}
   859→	//nolint:gosec // G302: Shell rc files need 0644 for user readability
   860→	f, err := os.OpenFile(rcFile, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
   861→	if err != nil {
   862→		return fmt.Errorf("opening file: %w", err)
   863→	}
   864→	defer f.Close()
   865→
   866→	_, err = f.WriteString("\n" + shellCompletionComment + "\n" + completionLine + "\n")
   867→	if err != nil {
   868→		return fmt.Errorf("writing completion: %w", err)
   869→	}
   870→	return nil
   871→}
   872→
   873→// promptTelemetryConsent asks the user if they want to enable telemetry.
   874→// It modifies settings.Telemetry based on the user's choice or flags.
   875→// The caller is responsible for saving settings.
   876→func promptTelemetryConsent(settings *EntireSettings, telemetryFlag bool) error {
   877→	// Handle --telemetry=false flag first (always overrides existing setting)
   878→	if !telemetryFlag {
   879→		f := false
   880→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   881→		return nil
   882→	}
   883→
   884→	// Skip if already asked
   885→	if settings.Telemetry != nil {
   886→		return nil
   887→	}
   888→
   889→	// Skip if env var disables telemetry (record as disabled)
   890→	if os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   891→		f := false
   892→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   893→		return nil
   894→	}
   895→
   896→	consent := true // Default to Yes
   897→	form := NewAccessibleForm(
   898→		huh.NewGroup(
   899→			huh.NewConfirm().
   900→				Title("Help improve Entire CLI?").
   901→				Description("Share anonymous usage data. No code or personal info collected.").
   902→				Affirmative("Yes").
   903→				Negative("No").
   904→				Value(&consent),
   905→		),
   906→	)
   907→
   908→	if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   909→		return fmt.Errorf("telemetry prompt: %w", err)
   910→	}
   911→
   912→	settings.Telemetry = &consent
   913→	return nil
   914→}
   915→
   916→// runUninstall completely removes Entire from the repository.
   917→func runUninstall(w, errW io.Writer, force bool) error {
   918→	// Check if we're in a git repository
   919→	if _, err := paths.RepoRoot(); err != nil {
   920→		fmt.Fprintln(errW, "Not a git repository. Nothing to uninstall.")
   921→		return NewSilentError(errors.New("not a git repository"))
   922→	}
   923→
   924→	// Gather counts for display
   925→	sessionStateCount := countSessionStates()
   926→	shadowBranchCount := countShadowBranches()
   927→	gitHooksInstalled := strategy.IsGitHookInstalled()
   928→	claudeHooksInstalled := checkClaudeCodeHooksInstalled()
   929→	geminiHooksInstalled := checkGeminiCLIHooksInstalled()
   930→	entireDirExists := checkEntireDirExists()
   931→
   932→	// Check if there's anything to uninstall
   933→	if !entireDirExists && !gitHooksInstalled && sessionStateCount == 0 &&
   934→		shadowBranchCount == 0 && !claudeHooksInstalled && !geminiHooksInstalled {
   935→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Entire is not installed in this repository.")
   936→		return nil
   937→	}
   938→
   939→	// Show confirmation prompt unless --force
   940→	if !force {
   941→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nThis will completely remove Entire from this repository:")
   942→		if entireDirExists {
   943→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - .entire/ directory")
   944→		}
   945→		if gitHooksInstalled {
   946→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Git hooks (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push)")
   947→		}
   948→		if sessionStateCount > 0 {
   949→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - Session state files (%d)\n", sessionStateCount)
   950→		}
   951→		if shadowBranchCount > 0 {
   952→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - Shadow branches (%d)\n", shadowBranchCount)
   953→		}
   954→		switch {
   955→		case claudeHooksInstalled && geminiHooksInstalled:
   956→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Claude Code, Gemini CLI)")
   957→		case claudeHooksInstalled:
   958→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Claude Code)")
   959→		case geminiHooksInstalled:
   960→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Gemini CLI)")
   961→		}
   962→		fmt.Fprintln(w)
   963→
   964→		var confirmed bool
   965→		form := NewAccessibleForm(
   966→			huh.NewGroup(
   967→				huh.NewConfirm().
   968→					Title("Are you sure you want to uninstall Entire?").
   969→					Affirmative("Yes, uninstall").
   970→					Negative("Cancel").
   971→					Value(&confirmed),
   972→			),
   973→		)
   974→
   975→		if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   976→			return fmt.Errorf("confirmation cancelled: %w", err)
   977→		}
   978→
   979→		if !confirmed {
   980→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "Uninstall cancelled.")
   981→			return nil
   982→		}
   983→	}
   984→
   985→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nUninstalling Entire CLI...")
   986→
   987→	// 1. Remove agent hooks (lowest risk)
   988→	if err := removeAgentHooks(w); err != nil {
   989→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove agent hooks: %v\n", err)
   990→	}
   991→
   992→	// 2. Remove git hooks
   993→	removed, err := strategy.RemoveGitHook()
   994→	if err != nil {
   995→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove git hooks: %v\n", err)
   996→	} else if removed > 0 {
   997→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Removed git hooks (%d)\n", removed)
   998→	}
   999→
  1000→	// 3. Remove session state files
  1001→	statesRemoved, err := removeAllSessionStates()
  1002→	if err != nil {
  1003→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove session states: %v\n", err)
  1004→	} else if statesRemoved > 0 {
  1005→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Removed session states (%d)\n", statesRemoved)
  1006→	}
  1007→
  1008→	// 4. Remove .entire/ directory
  1009→	if err := removeEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
  1010→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove .entire directory: %v\n", err)
  1011→	} else if entireDirExists {
  1012→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Removed .entire directory")
  1013→	}
  1014→
  1015→	// 5. Remove shadow branches
  1016→	branchesRemoved, err := removeAllShadowBranches()
  1017→	if err != nil {
  1018→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove shadow branches: %v\n", err)
  1019→	} else if branchesRemoved > 0 {
  1020→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Removed %d shadow branches\n", branchesRemoved)
  1021→	}
  1022→
  1023→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nEntire CLI uninstalled successfully.")
  1024→	return nil
  1025→}
  1026→
  1027→// countSessionStates returns the number of active session state files.
  1028→func countSessionStates() int {
  1029→	store, err := session.NewStateStore()
  1030→	if err != nil {
  1031→		return 0
  1032→	}
  1033→	states, err := store.List(context.Background())
  1034→	if err != nil {
  1035→		return 0
  1036→	}
  1037→	return len(states)
  1038→}
  1039→
  1040→// countShadowBranches returns the number of shadow branches.
  1041→func countShadowBranches() int {
  1042→	branches, err := strategy.ListShadowBranches()
  1043→	if err != nil {
  1044→		return 0
  1045→	}
  1046→	return len(branches)
  1047→}
  1048→
  1049→// checkClaudeCodeHooksInstalled checks if Claude Code hooks are installed.
  1050→func checkClaudeCodeHooksInstalled() bool {
  1051→	ag, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameClaudeCode)
  1052→	if err != nil {
  1053→		return false
  1054→	}
  1055→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
  1056→	if !ok {
  1057→		return false
  1058→	}
  1059→	return hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1060→}
  1061→
  1062→// checkGeminiCLIHooksInstalled checks if Gemini CLI hooks are installed.
  1063→func checkGeminiCLIHooksInstalled() bool {
  1064→	ag, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameGemini)
  1065→	if err != nil {
  1066→		return false
  1067→	}
  1068→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
  1069→	if !ok {
  1070→		return false
  1071→	}
  1072→	return hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1073→}
  1074→
  1075→// checkEntireDirExists checks if the .entire directory exists.
  1076→func checkEntireDirExists() bool {
  1077→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
  1078→	if err != nil {
  1079→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir
  1080→	}
  1081→	_, err = os.Stat(entireDirAbs)
  1082→	return err == nil
  1083→}
  1084→
  1085→// removeAgentHooks removes hooks from all agents that support hooks.
  1086→func removeAgentHooks(w io.Writer) error {
  1087→	var errs []error
  1088→
  1089→	// Remove Claude Code hooks
  1090→	claudeAgent, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameClaudeCode)
  1091→	if err == nil {
  1092→		if hookAgent, ok := claudeAgent.(agent.HookSupport); ok {
  1093→			wasInstalled := hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1094→			if err := hookAgent.UninstallHooks(); err != nil {
  1095→				errs = append(errs, err)
  1096→			} else if wasInstalled {
  1097→				fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Removed Claude Code hooks")
  1098→			}
  1099→		}
  1100→	}
  1101→
  1102→	// Remove Gemini CLI hooks
  1103→	geminiAgent, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameGemini)
  1104→	if err == nil {
  1105→		if hookAgent, ok := geminiAgent.(agent.HookSupport); ok {
  1106→			wasInstalled := hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1107→			if err := hookAgent.UninstallHooks(); err != nil {
  1108→				errs = append(errs, err)
  1109→			} else if wasInstalled {
  1110→				fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Removed Gemini CLI hooks")
  1111→			}
  1112→		}
  1113→	}
  1114→
  1115→	return errors.Join(errs...)
  1116→}
  1117→
  1118→// removeAllSessionStates removes all session state files and the directory.
  1119→func removeAllSessionStates() (int, error) {
  1120→	store, err := session.NewStateStore()
  1121→	if err != nil {
  1122→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to create state store: %w", err)
  1123→	}
  1124→
  1125→	// Count states before removing
  1126→	states, err := store.List(context.Background())
  1127→	if err != nil {
  1128→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to list session states: %w", err)
  1129→	}
  1130→	count := len(states)
  1131→
  1132→	// Remove the entire directory
  1133→	if err := store.RemoveAll(); err != nil {
  1134→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to remove session states: %w", err)
  1135→	}
  1136→
  1137→	return count, nil
  1138→}
  1139→
  1140→// removeEntireDirectory removes the .entire directory.
  1141→func removeEntireDirectory() error {
  1142→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
  1143→	if err != nil {
  1144→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir
  1145→	}
  1146→	if err := os.RemoveAll(entireDirAbs); err != nil {
  1147→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to remove .entire directory: %w", err)
  1148→	}
  1149→	return nil
  1150→}
  1151→
  1152→// removeAllShadowBranches removes all shadow branches.
  1153→func removeAllShadowBranches() (int, error) {
  1154→	branches, err := strategy.ListShadowBranches()
  1155→	if err != nil {
  1156→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to list shadow branches: %w", err)
  1157→	}
  1158→	if len(branches) == 0 {
  1159→		return 0, nil
  1160→	}
  1161→	deleted, _, err := strategy.DeleteShadowBranches(branches)
  1162→	return len(deleted), err
  1163→}
  1164→

input.content:
# Review 01

> Status: pending-dev
> Date: 2026-02-17
> Reviewer: Code Review Agent
> Verdict: APPROVE (with suggestions)

## Previous Review Status

No previous reviews.

## New Findings

### Critical (Must Fix)

None.

### Important (Should Fix)

1. **[cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go:31] Husky detection targets `.husky/_` but Husky v9+ restructured the directory layout.**
   The `_` subdirectory was a Husky v5-v8 implementation detail that contained the husky shell scripts. In Husky v9+, the `_` directory may not exist -- hooks are placed directly in `.husky/` (e.g., `.husky/pre-commit`). Detecting `.husky/_` will miss Husky v9 projects.

   Suggested fix: detect `.husky/` itself instead of `.husky/_`. The `.husky/` directory is the canonical indicator of Husky regardless of version. If there is a concern about false positives (some other tool creating `.husky/`), a secondary check for `.husky/_/husky.sh` (v5-v8) or any hook file in `.husky/` could confirm it.

   ```go
   // Detect .husky/ directory (present in all Husky versions)
   {filepath.Join(repoRoot, ".husky"), "Husky", ".husky/", true},
   ```

2. **[cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go:88-90] The `hookDir` variable is assigned from `m.ConfigPath` but only makes sense for Husky's directory-based structure.**
   The comment says "Extract the hook directory prefix from the config path" but `ConfigPath` is just the config indicator (e.g., `.husky/`). This works by coincidence for Husky (where `.husky/` is both the config path and the hook directory), but if another `OverwritesHooks: true` manager were added with a non-directory config path, the output would be confusing (e.g., `package.json/pre-commit:`).

   Suggested fix: Add a dedicated `HookDir` field to the `hookManager` struct, or add a comment clarifying this assumption is Husky-specific. Since only Husky has `OverwritesHooks: true` today, a comment is sufficient.

3. **[cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go:83-85] Warning message says "may overwrite hooks installed by Entire on npm install" -- this is Husky-specific language baked into a generic code path.**
   The `OverwritesHooks` branch uses `m.Name` for the tool name but hardcodes "npm install" as the trigger. If another tool that overwrites hooks via a different mechanism were added, the message would be misleading.

   Suggested fix: This is acceptable for now since only Husky has `OverwritesHooks: true`, but consider either adding a `TriggerDesc` field to `hookManager` or simply adding a code comment noting the Husky assumption.

### Suggestions (Consider)

1. **[cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go:41-46] The anonymous struct literal for Lefthook checks is verbose.**
   The 16 Lefthook permutation entries each repeat the full anonymous struct definition. This works but is harder to read than it needs to be.

   Suggested improvement: Define the struct type once as a named local type, or pull the Lefthook permutation generation into a helper. For example:

   ```go
   type hookCheck struct {
       path, name, configPath string
       overwritesHooks        bool
   }
   ```

   Then `checks` becomes `[]hookCheck{...}` and the append inside the loop becomes `checks = append(checks, hookCheck{...})`. Minor readability improvement.

2. **[cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers_test.go:420] The `initGitRepo` helper duplicates similar patterns in `hooks_test.go` and `common_test.go`.**
   There are multiple similar git-init test helpers across the strategy test files (e.g., `initTestRepo` in `common_test.go`, inline `git init` in `hooks_test.go`). This new `initGitRepo` adds another variation. Consider reusing `initTestRepo` from `common_test.go` (which also makes an initial commit), or at minimum add a comment explaining why a simpler version (no initial commit) was chosen here.

   Note: This is consistent with the existing pattern of per-file helpers in the codebase, so it is not blocking.

3. **[cmd/entire/cli/setup.go:722] The `setupGitHook` call writes to `os.Stderr` while the other 3 call sites write to the `w io.Writer` parameter.**
   This is because `setupGitHook` does not receive a writer parameter. It is not a new issue (the function predates this change), but worth noting that the warning destination is inconsistent. For the `entire enable git-hook` subcommand, the warning goes to stderr; for all other enable paths, it goes to stdout.

   This is arguably correct behavior (stderr for non-primary output) but inconsistent with the other 3 call sites.

4. **[cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go:139] `isLocalDev()` is called without error handling, which is consistent with the existing pattern in `hooks.go:181`.**
   No action needed -- just confirming this follows the established pattern.

5. **Consider adding Lefthook's `OverwritesHooks` flag.**
   Lefthook v1.4+ added `install` command behavior that can overwrite hooks (via `lefthook install`). Currently marked as `OverwritesHooks: false`, which is a reasonable default since Lefthook is more cooperative than Husky. But if users run `lefthook install` manually, Entire's hooks would be overwritten. The current "Note" message covers this adequately.

6. **[cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go:77-78] `buildHookSpecs` is called eagerly even for non-overwriting managers.**
   For the `OverwritesHooks: false` path, `specs` is allocated but never used. This is negligible performance-wise (4 small structs) but could be moved inside the `if m.OverwritesHooks` branch for clarity.

   ```go
   for _, m := range managers {
       if m.OverwritesHooks {
           specs := buildHookSpecs(cmdPrefix)
           // ... use specs
       } else {
           // ... note path
       }
   }
   ```

### Praise

- **Clean separation of concerns.** Detection (`detectHookManagers`), formatting (`hookManagerWarning`), and orchestration (`CheckAndWarnHookManagers`) are well-separated, making each function easy to test independently.

- **Thorough deduplication logic.** The `seen` map correctly handles the case where multiple Lefthook config variants exist simultaneously, reporting only once. This is a nice edge case to handle.

- **Comprehensive Lefthook format coverage.** All 16 permutations of `{.,}lefthook{,-local}.{yml,yaml,json,toml}` are correctly generated. This matches Lefthook's documented config file support.

- **Good test coverage.** 17 test functions covering all 4 managers individually, deduplication, multiple managers, both warning categories, empty/nil input, local dev prefix, the `extractCommandLine` utility, and end-to-end integration through `CheckAndWarnHookManagers`. The tests are well-structured with appropriate use of `t.Parallel()` and correct documentation of why the `CheckAndWarn*` tests cannot be parallel (due to `t.Chdir`).

- **Non-blocking integration pattern.** `CheckAndWarnHookManagers` silently returns on error (e.g., not in a git repo), which means hook manager detection never breaks the enable flow. This is the right design -- detection is advisory, not mandatory.

- **Consistent placement in setup.go.** All 4 call sites follow the same pattern: called immediately after `InstallGitHook` and before the success message. This ensures the warning appears at the right time in the user's output regardless of which enable path they take.

## Summary

**Overall assessment: APPROVE** with minor suggestions.

The implementation is well-designed, well-tested, and correctly integrated. The code follows Go idioms, uses proper error handling, and the test suite is thorough. The main substantive issue is the Husky detection targeting `.husky/_` which may miss Husky v9+ projects -- worth investigating before merge.

**Key concerns:**
- Husky v9+ detection may fail (Important #1)
- Minor code clarity improvements possible (Suggestions #1, #6)

**Estimated effort to address feedback:** 15-30 minutes for the Husky detection fix; the rest are optional improvements.

Here is the review summary:

---

## Review Complete

**Verdict: APPROVE** (with suggestions)

The review has been written to `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/docs/requirements/detect-hook-managers/review-01.md`.

### Key Findings

**Important (1 actionable item):**

The most significant issue is that **Husky detection targets `.husky/_`** (line 31 of `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go`), which was a Husky v5-v8 internal directory. Husky v9+ places hooks directly in `.husky/` without the `_` subdirectory. This means the detection will miss Husky v9 projects. The fix is straightforward -- detect `.husky/` instead of `.husky/_`.

**No Critical issues found.**

### What Was Done Well

- **Clean architecture**: Detection, formatting, and orchestration are properly separated into testable functions (`detectHookManagers`, `hookManagerWarning`, `CheckAndWarnHookManagers`).
- **Thorough test coverage**: 17 test functions covering all managers, deduplication, multiple managers, both warning categories, empty input, and end-to-end integration.
- **Correct Lefthook coverage**: All 16 config file permutations `{.,}lefthook{,-local}.{yml,yaml,json,toml}` are generated.
- **Non-blocking design**: Detection failures silently return, never breaking the `entire enable` flow.
- **Consistent integration**: All 4 call sites in `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/setup.go` follow the same pattern -- called after `InstallGitHook`, before the success message.

### Minor Suggestions

- Move `buildHookSpecs` call inside the `OverwritesHooks` branch to avoid unnecessary allocation.
- Extract the anonymous struct used for Lefthook checks into a named type for readability.
- Consider reusing `initTestRepo` from `common_test.go` instead of adding a new `initGitRepo` helper.

Tests all pass and lint is clean.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Review the changes on the current branch (feat/detect-hook-managers) compared to main. Focus on:
- Code quality and Go idioms
- Correctness of the detection logic
- Test coverage
- Integration with setup.go
- Any bugs or edge cases

The changes detect external hook managers (Husky, Lefthook, pre-commit, Overcommit) during `entire enable` and warn users.

Files changed:
- cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go (new)
- cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers_test.go (new)
- cmd/entire/cli/setup.go (modified - 4 call sites)

content.text:
Here is the review summary:

---

## Review Complete

**Verdict: APPROVE** (with suggestions)

The review has been written to `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/docs/requirements/detect-hook-managers/review-01.md`.

### Key Findings

**Important (1 actionable item):**

The most significant issue is that **Husky detection targets `.husky/_`** (line 31 of `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers.go`), which was a Husky v5-v8 internal directory. Husky v9+ places hooks directly in `.husky/` without the `_` subdirectory. This means the detection will miss Husky v9 projects. The fix is straightforward -- detect `.husky/` instead of `.husky/_`.

**No Critical issues found.**

### What Was Done Well

- **Clean architecture**: Detection, formatting, and orchestration are properly separated into testable functions (`detectHookManagers`, `hookManagerWarning`, `CheckAndWarnHookManagers`).
- **Thorough test coverage**: 17 test functions covering all managers, deduplication, multiple managers, both warning categories, empty input, and end-to-end integration.
- **Correct Lefthook coverage**: All 16 config file permutations `{.,}lefthook{,-local}.{yml,yaml,json,toml}` are generated.
- **Non-blocking design**: Detection failures silently return, never breaking the `entire enable` flow.
- **Consistent integration**: All 4 call sites in `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/setup.go` follow the same pattern -- called after `InstallGitHook`, before the success message.

### Minor Suggestions

- Move `buildHookSpecs` call inside the `OverwritesHooks` branch to avoid unnecessary allocation.
- Extract the anonymous struct used for Lefthook checks into a named type for readability.
- Consider reusing `initTestRepo` from `common_test.go` instead of adding a new `initGitRepo` helper.

Tests all pass and lint is clean.

## Review Summary

**APPROVE** with one important finding and a few minor suggestions:

### Important: Husky v9 detection
The detection checks for `.husky/_/` which is Husky v5-v8. Husky v9+ places hooks directly in `.husky/` without the `_` subdirectory. Should we update to detect `.husky/` instead?

### Minor suggestions
1. Move `buildHookSpecs` call inside the `OverwritesHooks` branch (avoids allocation when not needed)
2. Consider reusing existing `initTestRepo` helper instead of the new `initGitRepo`

Want me to apply the Husky v9 fix and the minor suggestions?

gh pr create --title "Detect external hook managers and warn during enable" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Detect Husky, Lefthook, pre-commit, and Overcommit during `entire enable` via filesystem checks (no file reads)
- Husky users get copy-paste instructions for durable hook integration (since Husky overwrites hooks on `npm install`)
- Other hook managers get a note to re-run `entire enable` if hooks are overwritten
- Lefthook detection covers all 16 valid config filenames (`{.,}lefthook{,-local}.{yml,yaml,json,toml}`)

## Test plan

- [x] `mise run fmt && mise run lint && mise run test:ci` passes
- [x] 17 unit tests covering all managers, dedup, warning output, edge cases
- [x] Manual test: temp repo with `.husky/_/` → warning with 4 hook commands shown
- [x] Manual test: temp repo with `lefthook.yml` → note shown
- [x] Manual test: clean repo → no warning

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

message.content: initGitRepo duplicates the existing initHooksTestRepo helper in hooks_test.go (both run git init, t.Chdir, and clear the RepoRoot cache). Reusing the shared helper would reduce duplication and keep repo setup logic consistent across tests.

content.text: `initHooksTestRepo` already does `t.TempDir()`, `t.Chdir()`, `git init`, and `ClearRepoRootCache()` — and returns `tmpDir`. I can replace the custom `initGitRepo` calls with it and drop the helper entirely.

message.content: should the tests in cmd/entire/cli/strategy/hook_managers_test.go use t.Parallel()?

content.thinking:
The user is asking about whether the tests should use `t.Parallel()`. Let me check the CLAUDE.md guidelines and the current state of the tests.

From CLAUDE.md:
> **Always use `t.Parallel()` in tests.** Every top-level test function and subtest should call `t.Parallel()` unless it modifies process-global state (e.g., `os.Chdir()`).
> 
> **Exception:** Tests that modify process-global state cannot be parallelized. This includes `os.Chdir()`/`t.Chdir()` and `os.Setenv()`/`t.Setenv()` — Go's test framework will panic if these are used after `t.Parallel()`.


The pure unit tests already have `t.Parallel()` correctly applied, while the integration-style tests that use `t.Chdir()` through `initHooksTestRepo` properly skip it since that function modifies process-global state and would cause a panic.

content.text:
The pure unit tests (`TestDetectHookManagers_*`, `TestHookManagerWarning_*`, `TestExtractCommandLine`) already do — that's correct.

The two `TestCheckAndWarnHookManagers_*` tests correctly **don't** — they call `initHooksTestRepo` which uses `t.Chdir()`, and per CLAUDE.md Go will panic if `t.Chdir()` is used after `t.Parallel()`. The comments already explain this.

So the current state is correct as-is.
